title: Reflow Oven Controller
bio: Redesigned an old toaster oven into a fully functional, multi-profile reflow oven.
description: This was our capstone project to end the electrical engineering degree. In a team of four, we designed and implemented a reflow oven. We used C++ for a multi-threaded firmware to program a Xinc2 microcontroller, using SPI protocols and a PID controller. The reflow oven sensed and displayed the temperature, humidity, and progress of reflow curve on an OLED. We set out to provide a few different reflow profile options based on the solder used. More in depth explanation can be found on GitHub.
challenges: Tuning the PID controller to maintain a precise reflow temperature curve without overshoot — small parameter changes had significant effects on solder quality.

Coordinating multi-threaded firmware tasks (temperature control, display updates, user input) on a resource-constrained microcontroller without race conditions. Using the XinC2 Microcontroller was part of our project constraints set by the client

title: MoneyMinder
bio: Budget and expense tracking app, using an Express.js backend connected to a MySQL server, RESTful APIs Gemini AI and Unsplash, and a responsive React.js frontend.
description: Budget and expense tracking app, using an Express.js backend connected to a MySQL server, RESTful APIs Gemini AI and Unsplash, and a responsive React.js frontend. Designed and developed in 10 days for the Brainstation course. I took the course to learn some new skills and new tech stack, so it was nice to see it all come together quickly.
challenges: Delivering a polished, full-stack product in 10 days required prioritization. Scoping the project early and resisting feature creep was the hardest design decision.
UI Design is not something that comes naturally to me, so the design phase took the most time.
Designing a database schema that could flexibly support budget categories, recurring transactions, and user accounts from day one saved significant refactoring time later.

title: Custom Macropad
bio: Designed and hand-soldered a custom macropad for my computer
description: Designed and hand-soldered a custom macropad for my computer. I added a small OLED, 16 programmable switches, and two rotary encoders.
challenges: Finding time for this project between work was difficult, but what I spent the most time on was soldering the PCB. I quickly learned how important it is to design with development and debugging in mind.

title: LDO PCB Design
bio: Designed and hand-soldered a Low Drop-Off PCB that taks a 15V input and outputs either 9V or 12V into the company's Spectrometer.
description: Designed and hand-soldered a Low Drop-Off PCB that taks a 15V input and outputs either 9V or 12V into the company's Spectrometer. Designed to be DIN-rail mountable to fit inside the spectrometer's table, and reduced noise.
challenges: This was my first PCB Design, so getting used to the new software and strict hardware considerations was the biggest challenge for this project.
These boards arrived at the office on my last week, so I worked hard to finish soldering one to test it before my term ended.

title: Offgassing Oven
bio: Built a temperature and pressure control circuit with a PID Controller, an RTD, solid state relay, and an AC/DC Converter.
description: Built a temperature and pressure control circuit with a PID Controller, an RTD, solid state relay, and an AC/DC Converter. This project started as a small, one-day project to regulate the temperature of a cylinder and clean U-Cups, but turned into a bigger project that could be used often. In the second iteration, I added a control box and a fuse to isolate the wires and improve safety. In the third iteration, I used LABView to control the temperature and PID parameters via MODBUS and RS485 communication protocols. Kicad was used to manage wiring schematics, and I maintained a BOM and documenation throughout the project.
challenges: LABView is a visual coding language so it was fun and tricky to learn a new way to interpret code.
